diary:
June 1 felt like one of those bright, easy days that still manages to keep you busy. I woke up in Renton Highlands with coffee brewing and the schedule open, then did that quick mental math for getting to the jobsite without wasting time. Starbucks at Downtown Renton hit the spot—mug in hand, mind already on check-ins.

At the site in Renton, I spent the morning walking the lines, staring at progress photos like they owed me answers, and juggling subcontractor timing. After lunch near QFC in Newport Hills, I swung by Staples in Renton Landing for pens and paper—my little letter habit is honestly what keeps me grounded.

The afternoon stayed sunny while I updated timelines and RFIs. After pickup basketball at Gene Coulon Memorial Beach Park, I headed downtown to The Crocodile in Seattle for the USA vs Wales World Cup coverage—wings, a drink, and that familiar “please don’t go sideways” feeling when the game got tense. I left early to dodge crowds, then came home and wrote letters until everything felt quiet again. Honestly, I’m tired—in a good way.
thought_process: I planned the day around friction points: the light rail closure between Westlake and University District made me stick to driving and build in a parking/check-in buffer at the jobsite. Keeping the morning efficient mattered because I had a couple urgent scheduling decisions with subcontractors that couldn’t wait. The sunny weather nudged me into a more physical break—lunch outside and basketball at Coulon felt like a reset rather than a detour. I grabbed office supplies deliberately; the handwritten letters are my anchor, especially after problem-solving all day. The World Cup coverage downtown at The Crocodile gave me a socially lighter outlet without committing to late crowds, so I watched, ate, then left early. By evening my mood shifted from “manage everything” to “hold steady,” and that made me calmer while writing.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
